campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth scholarships for recreation programs. The council also discussed extending a temporary local law and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including applying for grant funding to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth recreation scholarships
Extension of temporary local ordinance #2326
Hamilton Avenue bicycle bridge over Highway 17
Grant application for pedestrian and bike pathway funding

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to discuss a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved to approve the ordinance and scheduled it for final adoption at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community input
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived
Ordinance scheduled for consent calendar adoption at next meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda highlights

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various sessions held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting focused on administrative housekeeping items and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved multiple sets of meeting minutes from March 2026
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc. (project details not fully specified)
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction contract

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several important agreements and projects. The main topics included updating the airport's baggage system contract, protecting affordable housing if subsidy programs end, hiring a consultant to plan storm sewer improvements, and moving forward with a FEMA family camp project.
keyTopics: Airport baggage conveyor system upgrade contract
Affordable housing protections for residents if subsidies expire
Storm sewer system master planning
FEMA San José Family Camp project

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including decisions about major construction and improvement projects around town. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to honor young people and community volunteers.
keyTopics: Next year's budget for city construction and infrastructure projects
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
Capital improvement planning for 2026-27

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their April 7th, 2026 meeting, which included a flag salute to start. The council highlighted two important observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month, recognizing these communities and the importance of fair housing practices in our city.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Regular council meeting proceedings

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop to teach residents how to use OpenGov, a tool that lets the public see how the city spends its money. The council also discussed rules requiring people who lobby the city on specific issues to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demo for residents
City budget information now publicly accessible online
New lobbyist registration and reporting requirements
Rules for disclosing attempts to influence city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 covered legal disclosure requirements for residents. If you disagree with a city decision, you have 90 days from the decision date to file a lawsuit or legal challenge, otherwise you lose your right to challenge it in court.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over decisions
Legal rights for challenging city actions
California Code of Civil Procedure requirements

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several administrative items, including updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2026 and changes to how council vacancies are filled. The council also reviewed work requests from individual council members and approved meeting minutes.
keyTopics: Updates to city's 5-year federal funding plan (2025-2030 Consolidated Plan)
Changes to the council vacancy process through a new ordinance
Review of council member work requests and priorities
Approval of meeting minutes and calendar scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking to officially recognize the building as a historic landmark and wants permission to add 5,000 extra square feet of space as part of renovations to strengthen the building against earthquakes.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic landmark upgrade from Category 4 to Category 2
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for building renovations
Seismic safety improvements to existing building
Historic Resources Board meeting schedules and assignments
